---
title: Tiered Adapter
description: L1 (memory) + L2 (Redis) with promotion and write-through.
icon: Layers
---

## What is tiered caching?

The tiered adapter combines a fast **L1 in-memory** layer with a durable **L2 remote** layer (Redis, Upstash, etc.). On a read, it checks L1 first. If the entry is missing, it reads L2 and promotes the result into L1 for subsequent requests.

## Installation

```bash
bun add @justwant/cache ioredis
```

## Setup

```ts title="lib/cache.ts"
import { createCache } from "@justwant/cache";
import { createMemoryAdapter } from "@justwant/cache/adapters/memory";
import { createRedisAdapter } from "@justwant/cache/adapters/redis";
import { createTieredAdapter } from "@justwant/cache/adapters/tiered";
import Redis from "ioredis";

const redis = new Redis(process.env.REDIS_URL!);

export const cache = createCache({
  adapter: createTieredAdapter({
    l1: createMemoryAdapter({ maxSize: 1000 }),
    l2: createRedisAdapter({ client: redis }),
  }),
});
```

## Options

```ts
createTieredAdapter({
  l1: CacheAdapter;               // Fast local layer (required)
  l2: CacheAdapter;               // Durable remote layer (required)
  promote?: boolean;              // Promote L2 hits to L1 (default: true)
  writeThrough?: boolean;         // Write to both layers on set (default: true)
})
```

| Option | Default | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| `l1` | — | L1 adapter (memory) |
| `l2` | — | L2 adapter (Redis, Upstash…) |
| `promote` | `true` | Promote L2 hits to L1 on read |
| `writeThrough` | `true` | Write to both L1 and L2 on `set` |

## Read path

1. Check L1 — return if found and not expired.
2. Check L2 — if found, optionally promote to L1 and return.
3. Return `null` on miss.

## Write path

With `writeThrough: true` (default), every `set` writes to both L1 and L2. With `writeThrough: false`, only L2 is written — L1 is populated lazily on the next read.
